consumer_contexts: desktop, console, mobile, and spatial applications with player preferences
audio, display, controls, comfort, language, and accessibility configuration surfaces
common_kernel: typed setting definitions, values, defaults, scopes, and validation
whole-snapshot transactions with apply, reverse rollback, and persistence ports
discoverability metadata without claiming accessibility outcomes
configurable_variation: consumer-defined setting catalogs, applicators, profiles, categories, renderers, localization, and device-specific values
excluded_project_specific: one game's menu layout, labels, art, concrete quality tiers, comfort values, and product defaults
direct dependencies on a consumer scene, singleton, player service, private assembly, or vendor account
rationale: Typed player settings and transactional application recur across engines and products; the reusable kernel can remain independent of any particular menu, renderer, option catalog, default, or device.
non_claims: Admission and metadata do not prove accessibility, comfort, usability, localization quality, or named-device behavior.
